creator: hybridx87@gmail.com
text:
The problem is that he has crappy sound with his Maestro3 Sound Card.

error messages:
pcm0:play:1:dsp0.1: play interrupt timeout channel dead

% vmstat -i # GENERIC 6.2
interrupt                          total       rate 
irq0: clk                         151683        991 
irq1: atkbd0                         119          0 
irq5: pcm0                           469          3 
irq6: fdc0                             7          0 
irq7: ppc0                             1          0 
irq8: rtc                          19427        126 
irq10: cbb0 cbb1+*                  1444          9 
irq11: nvidia0                      7564         49 
irq12: psm0                         7089         46 
irq14: ata0                         7968         52 
Total                             195771       1279

this setup in /boot/loader.conf also did not helped:

snd_maestro3_load="YES"
hint.cbb.0.disabled="1" 
hint.cbb.1.disabled="1" 
hint.pccard.0.disabled="1" 
hint.pccard.1.disabled="1" 
hint.cardbus.0.disabled="1" 
hint.cardbus.1.disabled="1"

after removing pcimcia support from kernel:
% vmstat -i
interrupt                          total       rate 
irq0: clk                         206211        991 
irq1: atkbd0                         512          2 
irq5: pcm0                          1474          7 
irq6: fdc0                             7          0 
irq7: ppc0                             1          0 
irq8: rtc                          26408        126 
irq10: xl0 fwohci+                  4748         22 
irq11: nvidia0                      8684         41 
irq12: psm0                           36          0 
irq14: ata0                         7999         38 
Total                             256080       1231

after removing firewire from kernel:
% vmstat -i
interrupt                          total       rate 
irq0: clk                         247221        992 
irq1: atkbd0                         413          1 
irq5: pcm0                           509          2 
irq6: fdc0                             7          0 
irq7: ppc0                             1          0 
irq8: rtc                          31659        127 
irq10: xl0 uhci0                    6701         26 
irq11: nvidia0                     12729         51 
irq12: psm0                            6          0 
irq14: ata0                         9677         38 
Total                             308923       1240

no IRQ conflicts at this time, but the glitchy sound still is the problem.

setting hint.pcm.0.hwvol_config="0" in /boot/loader.conf also did not helped.

hardware:

Dell Latitude C810 BIOS A12

P3 Tualatin Mobile 1000/733 133MHz FSB 
2x256 SDRAM 133MHz Kingston 
Intel 815e/ep 
Geforce2Go 16MB DDR 
ESS Maestro 3 
3Com 56k V.90Mini PCI Modem/ 3Com Ethernet 10/100 
Kontroler IEEE 1394 Texas Instruments 
CardBUS Texas Instruments PCI-4451 
Dysk Toshiba MK2018GAS 
CD SAMSUNG SN-124

kernel was GENERIC 6.2[later with remover pcimcia and firewire] also 7.0/5.4 GENERIC tried without success.

here is some other info:

% pciconf -lv

% vmstat -i # from GENERIC FreeBSD 7.0-CURRENT 200702 snapshot
interrupt                          total       rate 
irq0: clk                        1713674        999

the other problem is also that booting in SAFE MODE or with ACPI DISABLED the laptop is not booting and bootloader is showing that it cant load the kernel.


thanks for any help and hints.

Fix: 

i dont known but in windows, linux 2.6 2.4 ist OK
How-To-Repeat: 1.run xmms and play any song;)
2 run next copy xmms and click play song
3 ok play two copy xmms - its ok but..
4 if any copy xmms click stop -sound crappy sometime system halt
5 on tty0 i see:
pcm0:play:1:dsp0.1: play interrupt timeout channel dead
